user.title: Set up your Fire TV
user.description:
Enter your Fire TV connection details below.

**How to find your IP address:**
Settings → My Fire TV → About → Network → IP Address

**How to enable ADB Debugging:**
Settings → My Fire TV → Developer Options → ADB Debugging → ON

Both devices must be on the same Wi-Fi network.

data_description.port: Default is 5555, only change if you modified it
data_description.name: A friendly name for your dashboard

confirm_tv.title: Approve on your TV
confirm_tv.description:
When you click **Submit**, a USB debugging prompt will appear on your Fire TV screen.

**You must do these two things:**
1. Check the box **"Always allow from this computer"**
2. Select **Allow** using your Fire TV remote

You have 30 seconds to approve. Make sure your TV is on and remote is nearby.

**Important:** Checking "Always allow" ensures you won't be prompted again when saving settings later.

error.cannot_connect: Connection failed. Make sure you approved the prompt on your TV within 30 seconds, the IP is correct, and ADB Debugging is enabled. Click Submit to try again.

abort.already_configured: This Fire TV is already configured.

init.title: Fire TV Enhanced Settings
init.description:
No TV approval needed to save settings.

**Installed apps detected:**
{app_list}

✅ = custom name, • = auto-detected

data_description.scan_interval: How often to check which app is running. 1s = fastest but uses more CPU. Default: 5s
data_description.screenshot_interval: How often to capture a screenshot. 1s = real-time but uses more bandwidth. Default: 10s
data_description.custom_apps_text:
To rename an app, copy the package name from the list above and add:
package.name = Your Name

One per line. Example:
com.apple.atv = Apple TV
tv.twitch.android.viewer = Twitch
